framework.name: Layered Authorization Architecture (OAuth vs Policy Engine)
framework.description: Distinguishes between authentication/token issuance layer (OAuth 2.1) and policy enforcement layer (Oso/OPA). OAuth answers 'who is this and what may they do' broadly; policy engines answer 'for this specific actor and resource, is this action permitted' granularly. Suggests authorization is not monolithic but requires coordination between identity and policy layers.
framework.raw_quote: OAuth 2.1 is concerned with getting a valid access token. The token is attached to an identity; Oso determines if the tool that's requested is actually allowed access by the identity. In other words, OAuth decides 'who is this and what may they do.' Oso answers, 'For this specific actor and this specific resource, this action (or no action) is permitted.'

source.title: Best Practices of Authorizing AI Agents - Oso
